reasoning: The tragic passing of Kelvin Kiptum in February 2024 removed the only athlete realistically poised to break the 2-hour barrier in the near term. The next fastest living runner, Eliud Kipchoge, is near 40 and has shown signs of decline. The rest of the elite field holds personal bests no faster than 2:01:48, a massive gap of nearly two minutes from the 1:59:59 requirement. Furthermore, initial super-shoe technological advantages have already been realized. With only a few fast major marathon windows remaining before the end of 2025, the likelihood of a new generational talent dropping roughly two minutes below the current living field's average best is exceptionally low.
